title: A royal family dined at a popular Somerville restaurant this week
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Belgium’s royal family dined at Sarma in Somerville, celebrating Princess Elisabeth's Harvard graduation.
Sarma in Somerville, known for Mediterranean fare, hosted Belgian royalty but details of their meal remain undisclosed.
Princess Elisabeth graduated from Harvard with a master's in public policy, planning an Atlantic sailing trip post-graduation.

title: What to know about the rejection of Healey’s Parole Board nominee as backlash builds
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor's Council rejected Maura Healey's Parole Board nominee Vincent DeMore despite support from district attorneys.
DeMore, a defense attorney and former prosecutor, was opposed due to concerns over his prosecutorial background.
The board's decision follows scrutiny after a parolee's involvement in a Cambridge shooting, raising public safety concerns.

title: Mike Vrabel talks offseason distractions, potential trades, and more
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots' coach Mike Vrabel addressed offseason distractions, emphasizing focus on coaching and balancing family and football responsibilities.
Vrabel did not confirm rumors of a trade potentially bringing A.J. Brown from the Eagles to New England.
Vrabel missed Day 3 of the NFL Draft for personal reasons, impacting Patriots' draft strategy led by Eliot Wolf.

title: Video shows Sullivan’s Tap patrons running for cover when shots fired near TD Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Shots were fired near TD Garden Tuesday night; no injuries reported, according to Boston Police.
Sullivan’s Tap patrons ran for cover after gunshots around 10:45 p.m.; video shared on Instagram.
Police found ballistic damage to a vehicle and a shattered Hotel Indigo door; investigation ongoing.

title: Wu cancels Harvard speaking engagement rather than cross grad-student picket line
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu canceled her Harvard Law School speech to avoid crossing a grad-student picket line.
The Harvard Graduate Students Union has been striking since April 21 over contract negotiation issues.
Wu's team tried to reach a compromise with the union, but all proposals were declined.

title: Mike’s Pastry is coming to Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Mike's Pastry will open a new location in Terminal C at Logan Airport later this year.
The airport branch will feature filled-to-order cannoli, cheesecake slices, lobster tails, and a full espresso bar.
Open daily from 5 a.m. to 10 p.m., the bakery will offer travelers a taste of Boston's North End.

title: ‘Stranger Things’ actor Joe Keery joins star-studded cast for Broadway debut
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Joe Keery and Julia Louis-Dreyfus will debut on Broadway in 'Other Desert Cities' alongside Allison Janney, Ed Harris, and Lily Rabe.
'Other Desert Cities', a Tony Award-winning play, will run for a 16-week limited engagement at Hudson Theatre from Sept. 29, 2026.
The play, about a politically connected Palm Springs family, was a finalist for the 2012 Pulitzer Prize in Drama.

title: Police close South Boston beach after ‘crowds of youth’ break into fights
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Carson Beach in South Boston closed after multiple youth fights on Tuesday evening, police report.
Massachusetts State Police responded to reports of altercations, loud music, and alcohol use starting around 5:30 p.m.
The area was cleared by 9:30 p.m. with no injuries or arrests, and Day Boulevard has reopened.

title: Clover says it’s closing for good
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Clover Food Lab is closing all 11 locations on May 28 due to financial difficulties and inability to find a buyer.
The vegetarian chain, which originated as a food truck in 2008 at MIT, faced rising costs and increased competition.
Clover emerged from bankruptcy in 2024, but could not sustain operations amid inflation and pressure on profit margins.

title: Here are the local Mass. ties on the U.S. World Cup roster
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Goalkeeper Matt Turner returns to New England for more playtime in bid to rejoin the U.S. World Cup team.
Matt Freese, Harvard alumnus, could become the second former Harvard player to play in a World Cup.
Miles Robinson, from Arlington, is included in U.S. roster after recovering from an Achilles injury.
